title: dactylorhiza umbrosa subsp. persica
intro: Dactylorhiza umbrosa subsp. persica, also known as the Persian orchid, is a subspecies of the Dactylorhiza umbrosa plant, a type of terrestrial orchid native to the mountainous regions of Central Asia and the Caucasus. The Persian orchid has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful in the treatment of various health conditions. The health benefits of Dactylorhiza umbrosa subsp. persica include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve cardiovascular health, and boost the immune system. In herbal preparations, the Persian orchid is often used in the form of dried roots, which can be infused in hot water to make a tea, or used in combination with other herbs to create a tincture or decoction.
